Philipp Bauer, [14 Feb 2026 at 12:09:13]:
...enjoyed reading the article. 

The table of hosting providers, their pitch and the reality is helpful to know where to start looking.

Great explanation of vLLM and llama.cpp and why / when to use it. I’m using both in practice myself

On the part of model IP, I think it would be worth highlighting what the risks are. As someone who isn’t close to the subject it is probably not obvious what the implications are 

I get headaches thinking about a split UK/EU GDPR but routing requests like you explain is a cool way around it


Thanks Philipp for the feedback. I’ll add some further thoughts outlining the actual scope of the risks on IP.

https://berget.ai/

I believe they are similar to Civo - although I havent looked at Civos services in detail.

I'm writing an article that will partly be about Berget AI from the angle that the Swedish Central Bank choose them for their more sensitive inference


Thank you Robert! I shall do some research!

Furthermore, you get a provider that must comply with the same legislation that applies to you, meaning much of the documentation will be prepared by the provider in a way that's already adapted to Swedish conditions.\"\n\n### Jim Runsten\n\nCEO Synch Law Firm\n\n\"Berget's approach with reused hardware is smart from both a sustainability and security perspective. By building the system with the assumption that things can break, vulnerability is reduced.
